name: Firmware Supply Chain Attack
description: Attack tree modelling UEFI/BIOS-level firmware supply chain compromise for persistent, OS-independent implants that survive reimaging and disk replacement
root_objective: Implant persistent firmware-level backdoor via the hardware supply chain

nodes.title: Deploy Persistent Firmware Implant
nodes.description: Install a UEFI/BIOS-level implant that persists across OS reinstalls, disk replacements, and provides undetectable remote access

nodes.title: Compromise UEFI Firmware Update
nodes.description: Insert malicious code into the UEFI firmware distributed to endpoints

nodes.title: Compromise OEM Build Pipeline
nodes.description: Infiltrate the firmware build system of a major OEM (Dell, HP, Lenovo) to inject a UEFI rootkit

nodes.title: Trojanise BIOS Update Utility
nodes.description: Modify the firmware flash utility distributed on the vendor's support website to include a rootkit

nodes.title: Exploit UEFI Vulnerabilities
nodes.description: Exploit bugs in UEFI firmware to install an implant post-deployment

nodes.title: Exploit SPI Flash Write Vulnerability
nodes.description: Bypass SPI flash write protections to directly modify the UEFI firmware image in place

nodes.title: Abuse Capsule Update Mechanism
nodes.description: Craft a malicious UEFI capsule update that exploits insufficient signature verification

nodes.title: Intercept Hardware in Transit
nodes.description: Physically modify hardware during the supply chain to install firmware implants

nodes.title: Interdict Shipments
nodes.description: Intercept computer shipments during transit to flash modified firmware before delivery

nodes.title: Compromise Contract Manufacturer
nodes.description: Infiltrate the contract manufacturer's factory to modify firmware during production

nodes.title: Maintain Firmware Persistence
nodes.description: Ensure the implant persists across updates and security scans

nodes.title: Hook Secure Boot Chain
nodes.description: Modify the Secure Boot certificate database to trust the implant's signing key

nodes.title: Implant in SMM (System Management Mode)
nodes.description: Hide implant in SMM memory space which is invisible to the OS and most security tools
